rows.source_ref: "Trading Strategy Bladerunner", posted by ruben-trader (copy-paste of Erron's article, AuthenticFX), ForexFactory thread 604020, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/604020 ; original source authenticfx.com free-forex-... (Bladerunner strategy)
rows.concept: Trend continuation: use the 20 EMA (or Bollinger mid-band) as a polarity line and enter on a successful retest/rejection of the EMA in the direction price is currently respecting.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 codeable (20 EMA + candlestick logic), deterministic SL (2 pips beyond signal candle) and 1R/2R TP with breakeven trail; FX M5 data available; FTMO/DXZ-safe (fixed stops, no grid). Core mechanic testable but relies on discretionary overlays (breakout-of-consolidation precondition, S/R + round-number + fundamentals confluence, 'convincing' close).
rows.notes: Generic 20-EMA pullback/retest trend-continuation, one of countless similar; author explicitly warns no entry on naked EMA rejection alone (needs confluence/fundamentals we cannot mechanize) -> low mechanical-edge plausibility, no unique return driver. Variants (ONE row): (1) single order vs two split orders (TP1=1R closes half, remainder to breakeven), (2) 20 EMA vs Bollinger 20 mid-band vs combined 'forex polarity indicator' ribbon. Buy/sell-stop 2 pips beyond confirmatory candle, orders expire at next candle open. Built-in news filter: no entry 30-45 min before scheduled news, wait 15 min after. Forum content is an acknowledged copy-paste of Erron/AuthenticFX -> reputability caveat, lowers priority. Pages 6-7 are only thread chatter.
